name: linux-smart-enumeration
summary: First, a couple of useful oneliners ;)

documentation.readme: Note that since version 2.10 you can serve the script to other hosts with the -S flag!

arguments.name: -c
arguments.description: Disable color

arguments.name: -e
arguments.description: Comma separated list of paths to exclude. This allows you

arguments.name: -h
arguments.description: This help

arguments.name: -i
arguments.description: Non interactive mode

arguments.name: -l
arguments.description: Output verbosity level

arguments.name: -p
arguments.description: Time that the process monitor will spend watching for

arguments.name: -S
arguments.description: Serve the lse.sh script in this host so it can be retrieved

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --port
arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: Number of concurrent workers.

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: Per-operation timeout in seconds.

arguments.name: --rate
arguments.description: Packet / request rate limit (packets/sec).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --user-agent
arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header value.

arguments.name: --cookie
arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header value.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output instead of human-readable text.

arguments.name: --no-color
arguments.description: Disable ANSI color in output.

arguments.name: --quiet
arguments.description: Suppress non-error output.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --yes
arguments.description: Skip confirmation prompts (use with care).

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

arguments.name: --no-banner
arguments.description: Suppress banner / tool-name output.

arguments.name: --capture
arguments.description: Capture mode: write packets to a pcap file.

function_signatures.description: Top-level entry point for linux-smart-enumeration (diego-treitos/linux-smart-enumeration). Reads CLI args, dispatches to subcommands.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with volatilityfoundation/volatility3 (score=10); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [shell_linux] in the Penetration Testing category. Fits when the engagement needs: First, a couple of useful oneliners ;).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Note that since version 2.10 you can serve the script to other hosts with the -S flag!.
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `wget "$KFIOSA_TARGET_URL" -O lse.sh;chmod 700 lse.sh`. Further invocations from upstream: `curl "$KFIOSA_TARGET_URL" -Lo lse.sh;chmod 700 lse.sh`; `Use: ./lse.sh [options]`.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: Use during the active exploitation phase once recon has identified a service or surface on the engagement host.
Use as a fallback when the primary exploit path (CVE match, KB hit) did not yield a shell.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Pair with the LLM system-prompt stanza to ensure the chain planner knows how to call this tool.
